risk: A file editor can destroy or normalize user Engine.ini content
mitigation: Implemented lossless merge, external-change guard, byte backup, atomic replace, readback hash verification, recovery journals, and failure tests; Windows filesystem runtime verification remains required

risk: High or Realtime priority can reduce system responsiveness
mitigation: Implemented Normal default, no automatic dangerous selection, persistent accessible warnings and tooltips, explicit confirmation, native readback, and exact restoration; Windows behavior remains a release gate

risk: Updater signing key loss or exposure breaks the update trust chain
mitigation: Protected secrets, encrypted offline recovery, draft release, mandatory signature and tamper tests

risk: Game or account action may result from an unofficial third-party tool
mitigation: No injection/hooks/bypass, current terms link, explicit bilingual acknowledgement, no safety guarantee, seek written Kuro guidance before broad promotion

risk: A Kuro character-derived app icon may fall outside fan-work permission for software
mitigation: The shipped raster and complete Tauri icon set use a completely original non-character [T] monogram; generated character exploration is excluded from the repository and release

risk: Community Engine.ini claims may be ignored or regress after patches
mitigation: Evidence states, tested-version metadata, promotion protocol, no automatic reapply after external/game patch change

risk: macOS development host cannot directly verify Windows APIs and NSIS runtime
mitigation: Windows GitHub Actions, helper-process integration tests, clean Windows VM release gate

risk: A global background-priority mode can cause audio dropouts, recording loss, input lag, application instability, or restoration of the wrong PID
mitigation: Implemented default-off preview and confirmation, adaptive measured contention, same-user/session eligibility, pinned user exclusions, hard critical/foreground/audio/communication/recording exclusions, PID+creation-time+image+applied-value restore guards, durable journals, and no registry or power-plan mutation; Windows process/audio runtime verification remains required

risk: Cache cleanup can delete user data through path confusion or disrupt other games by clearing shared NVIDIA shader caches
mitigation: Exact-leaf allowlist; validated game/current-user roots; drive-to-root and descendant no-follow handle pins; stable identity and approved-manifest deletion; preview counts, bytes, typed rebuild/no-backup warnings and explicit confirmation; no LocalStorage/Config/SaveGames; NVIDIA shared-cache warning; no process termination or elevation; per-target partial and crash receipts; Windows runtime gate remains required

risk: Treating WuWa as universally single-core-bound or reserving too many cores can reduce performance on different CPU topologies and game versions
mitigation: Community claim is not promoted to verified fact; measure per-core saturation; soft CPU Sets only; topology and minimum-headroom gates; no default 1-2-core hard affinity; preview/readback/restore; Windows hardware-matrix benchmarks before release

risk: A privileged Tauri command or stale preview can write an unintended file, race cache maintenance, or expose local paths to the frontend
mitigation: Backend-owned validated installation roots and dialogs; opaque bounded preview tokens bound to hashes, target sets, process epochs and installation generations; shared exclusive launch/write/cache/update/shutdown gate; fail-closed game-state revalidation; typed sanitized errors; restrictive CSP/capabilities and server-owned external links; no broad frontend filesystem or shell access. Windows runtime and clean-VM gates remain required.
